Dry Bulk Fixture Report and Baltic Dry Indices for Monday 15.12.2025

All the above is given in good faith but wog. E&OE.
WRITEUP
CAPESIZE
The market began the week on a cautiously positive note, with the BCI 5TC edging up by $110 to $30,841. Overall, it was a mixed yet constructive start. Two miners were active in the Pacific, lending support to sentiment, and fixtures were reported in the $10.50–$10.75 range, with market talk suggesting levels as high as $10.90 for earlier arrivals. Reflecting this firmer tone, the C5 index rose by 0.330 to close at $10.820. In contrast, the Atlantic saw a slower start, although activity picked up as the day progressed. South Brazil and West Africa to China opened on a more positive footing, with underlying sentiment showing tentative improvement. On C3, bids for index dates were reported in the low to mid $22s, while offers remained closer to $23.00, although the index nonetheless advanced by 0.269 to $22.155. Later in the day, fixtures were reported for 12 January onwards on C3 at $23.00–$23.40. Meanwhile, the North Atlantic remained relatively muted, with sentiment described as softer as the week got under way.
Atlantic
Norden is reported to have fixed RWE TBN Newcastlemax for 190,000/10 from Tubarao option West Africa to Qingdao 12/17 January at $23.00. Cargill fixed a OTSL TBN for 170,000-190,000/10 from Tubarao option West Africa to Qingdao 12/17 January at $23.40. Classic is reported to have fixed the Costamare controlled Mildred (179,678 2011) for 170,000/10 from Tubarao to Qingdao 17/22 January at either $22.88 or $22.95, lacking further details.
Asia
Rio Tinto fixed a TBN for 170,000/10 from Dampier to Qingdao for 29/31 December at $10.75 and were also reported to have paid $10.90 for eta 27 December. BHP fixed a TBN for 160,000/10 from Port Hedland to Qingdao for 30 December/1 January at $10.50.
PANAMAX
The week opened on a softer footing, with sentiment continuing to ease amid limited Atlantic and Pacific fixture activity. Both trans Atlantic and fronthaul rates appear to be under pressure as charterers persist in testing lower levels, with some resistance from owners beginning to soften. In Asia, an ample supply of prompt tonnage has weighed on the market, allowing charterers to circulate increasingly aggressive bids in the hope of drawing owners into early cover. Period activity remained muted, and the P5TC average declined by $398 to close at $14,796.
Atlantic
The scrubber fitted Aquagrace (81,790 2017) was rumoured to have fixed and failed with Messrs Raffles basis delivery EC South America 5/10 January for a trip to Singapore-Japan at $16,000+$600,000 bb. The Great Wealth (75,570 2011) which sailed from Ennore 2 December was said to be fixed for a fronthaul, but further details lacked. The Nord Auriga (81,795 2020) open in Praia Mole 18 December was also said to have fixed but full details are yet to surface.
Asia
The Prestige D (95,709 2011) open in Huanghua 11/15 December fixed for an Indonesia into Japan, but a rate did not emerge. The same can be said for the Seacon Oslo (85,688 2023) open Tianjin 17/18 December where further details lacked.
SUPRAMAX
A subdued start to the week as further erosion was seen within the Atlantic. The US Gulf was described as lacking fresh enquiry and rates both for fronthaul and trans Atlantic runs came under downward pressure. Activity was limited elsewhere although it surfaced that the Xin Hai Tong 62 (56,709 2012) open San Pedro (W Africa) 19/20 December was fixed for a trip redelivery Red Sea at $23,000, but no more details came to light. From Asia, again negative sentiment prevailed certainly from the south, although some felt that there was a little more enquiry from the north. The 11TC average closed down $144 at $17,189.
HANDYSIZE
Not surprisingly in the last full working week for many prior to the seasonal holidays and New Year, it was a rather dull start to the week. Sentiment turned rather negative across the board as both the Atlantic and Pacific basins took stock to see what actually needed to be fixed prior to the long break. Activity remained limited from both the North and South Atlantic with limited fresh enquiry coming into play. Whilst the Continent-Mediterranean also lost a little ground although rates remained reasonable for the time of year brokers said. The Asian arena also remained negative again some saying that fresh impetus would be needed to see any change in fortunes. There was talk of period activity it surfaced that the SF Darika (35,283 2010) open Bristol 15/21 December was heard fixed at around $14,500 for 3/5 months trading but no more details came to light. The 7 TC average closed down $153 to finish the day at $14,329.
All the above is given in good faith but wog. E&OE.
Thanks and best regards.
Anand S Mehta
